growth_records.strain: multiple isolates (exact n and per-strain % not given in main text)

up.method: calculated: species range 11-42% of 0.05 g/100mL urea (=8.325 mmol/L) hydrolyzed over 24 h; rate=(fraction x 8.325 mmol/L)/24h

growth_records.cite: Liu et al. Functional gene-guided enrichment plus in situ microsphere cultivation enables isolation of new crucial ureolytic bacteria from the rumen of cattle. Microbiome (2023). DOI:10.1186/s40168-023-01510-4. [PMC10105427]
growth_records.snippet: Strains of K. pneumoniae, C. koseri, C. amalonaticus, and A. butzleri hydrolyzed 20-54%, 11-42%, 2-27%, and 13-28%, respectively, of the urea added to the medium.
growth_records.notes: Range reflects variation among multiple C. koseri strains isolated in this study (per-strain breakdown only in Fig. 6B). Rate=(%hydrolyzed x 8.325 mmol/L urea)/24h, same assay conditions as S99 record. Volumetric, not gDW-normalized.

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 4330 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/4330

phenotypes.base_medium: Enzyme / activity assay (hydrolysis, degradation, reduction, electron transfer) in a test medium — not a sole-substrate growth test
